prerequisites: No external account, credentials, or network access are required. DataGen generates synthetic test data locally and does not connect to any external system.
Decide the data shape via the required flavor field (the data generation type/pattern, for example Incremental).
Decide the required max_records value (the total number of records to generate).

authMethods.name: No authentication
authMethods.summary: This connector requires no authentication. The descriptor exposes no secret fields, and the vendor docs state that DataGen generates data locally and does not connect to any external systems, so no API key, token, or credentials are needed.

setupSteps.title: Add the DataGen source
setupSteps.body: In Airbyte, go to Sources, click New source, and select End-to-End Testing (DataGen). Enter a name for the source.

setupSteps.title: Choose the data generation flavor
setupSteps.body: Set the required flavor field to the data generation type/pattern you want the connector to emit (for example Incremental). This controls the shape of the synthetic records.

setupSteps.title: Set the record volume
setupSteps.body: Set the required max_records field to the total number of records to generate.

setupSteps.title: Tune concurrency (optional)
setupSteps.body: Optionally set concurrency to control the maximum number of concurrent data generators. Leave it empty to use the default.

setupSteps.title: Save the source
setupSteps.body: Click Set up source. No connection test against an external system is needed because the data is generated locally.
